fields.title: Object Oriented Analysis and Design Lab
Problem Solving and Python Programming Lab
Question Paper Assignment
fields.message: You have been assigned to set End Semester question paper for CS23201 - Object Oriented Programming. Deadline: 2026-02-14

fields.co1_description: CO1: Understand the basic principles of object-oriented programming
fields.co2_description: CO2: Apply object-oriented concepts to solve programming problems
fields.co3_description: CO3: Analyze and design classes, inheritance hierarchies, and polymorphism
fields.co4_description: CO4: Implement exception handling and file operations
fields.co5_description: CO5: Evaluate and apply advanced OOP concepts in real-world applications

fields.question_text: Define Object-Oriented Programming. List its key features.
What is a class? How does it differ from an object?
Explain the concept of encapsulation with an example.
What is inheritance? List its types.
Define polymorphism. Differentiate between compile-time and runtime polymorphism.
What is method overloading? Give an example.
Explain the purpose of constructor in a class.
What is an abstract class? How is it different from an interface?
Define exception handling. List the keywords used in exception handling.
What is a package? Explain its advantages.
(a) Explain the concept of classes and objects with a suitable example program. Demonstrate how to create multiple objects and access their members. (13 marks)
(b) Describe the different types of constructors in Java. Write a program demonstrating default constructor, parameterized constructor, and copy constructor. (13 marks)
(a) Explain different types of inheritance with examples. Implement a Java program demonstrating multilevel inheritance with appropriate method access. (13 marks)
(b) What is method overriding? Explain with a suitable example. Discuss the use of 'super' keyword in method overriding and constructor chaining. (13 marks)
(a) Explain abstract classes and interfaces in Java. Write a program implementing an interface and demonstrate multiple interface implementation. (13 marks)
(b) What is polymorphism? Explain compile-time and runtime polymorphism with complete Java programs demonstrating both concepts. (13 marks)
(a) Describe exception handling mechanism in Java. Explain try-catch-finally blocks with examples. Write a program handling multiple exceptions. (13 marks)
(b) Explain user-defined exceptions in Java. Write a program that creates a custom exception class and demonstrates throwing and catching custom exceptions. (13 marks)
(a) Explain file handling in Java. Write a program to read data from a file and write modified data to another file using FileInputStream and FileOutputStream. (13 marks)
(b) What are Java Collections? Explain ArrayList and HashMap with examples. Write a program demonstrating basic operations on ArrayList. (13 marks)

CASE STUDY:

A university library management system needs to be developed using Object-Oriented Programming concepts. The system should manage books, students, and borrowing operations.

Requirements:
- The library has different types of books: Textbooks, Reference Books, and Journals
- Each book has attributes: ISBN, title, author, publisher, and availability status
- Students can borrow books (maximum 3 at a time) and return them
- Fine calculation: Rs. 2 per day for late returns (due date is 14 days from borrowing)
- The system should track borrowing history

Using the above scenario:
(i) Design an appropriate class hierarchy showing inheritance relationships between Book types (5 marks)
(ii) Implement the Library class with methods for issuing and returning books using exception handling for invalid operations (5 marks)  
(iii) Demonstrate polymorphism by implementing a display method that shows different information for different book types (5 marks)
